note_html:
Subjective:

HPI: The patient, Matthew, has been experiencing lower back pain for an extended period, with a significant exacerbation of symptoms over the past month. The pain is localized to the lower back area, radiating down the left leg, and is described as an electric toothache-like sensation. The pain is exacerbated by standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing, but is somewhat alleviated by lying down. The patient reports a pain level of 6 out of 10 currently, but can reach up to 13 out of 10 when symptoms worsen. The patient has been unable to perform daily activities due to pain, requiring the use of a cane.

Past medical history: The patient mentions a past injury 30 years ago, where they fell off a roof, which may have resulted in a fracture or possible nerve damage. However, this is not further clarified.

Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in both legs, with weakness in the lower extremities. Coughing and sneezing exacerbate the symptoms.

Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]

Objective:

Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back. The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg. Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.

Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.

Assessment / Problem List:

Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and radiculopathy.

Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; radiculopathy; lower back pain; leg pain.

Plan:

I recommended an epidural steroid injection to be administered today to alleviate the inflammation and pain. I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area. The patient is expected to follow up with me in about a month to assess the efficacy of the treatment.

Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in about a month to assess the efficacy of the treatment.
